title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. subsecundus
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. subsecundus, also known as slough grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Elymus genus and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Elymus trachycaulus subsp. subsecundus as a medicinal plant.
